questions.question: What is an ecosystem?
questions.correct_answer: A system where living organisms interact with each other and with their physical environment.
questions.explanation: An ecosystem includes both living (biotic) and non-living (abiotic) components working together.

questions.question: What are biotic components?
questions.correct_answer: The living components of an ecosystem such as plants, animals and microorganisms.
questions.explanation: Biotic components interact with each other and depend on abiotic factors for survival.

questions.question: What are abiotic components?
questions.correct_answer: The non-living components of an ecosystem such as air, water, soil and sunlight.
questions.explanation: Abiotic factors influence the survival and growth of organisms.

questions.question: Who are producers in an ecosystem?
questions.correct_answer: Organisms that make their own food using sunlight through photosynthesis.
questions.explanation: Green plants and algae are producers and form the base of food chains.

questions.question: What is a food chain?
questions.correct_answer: A sequence of organisms where each organism feeds on the one before it.
questions.explanation: It shows the transfer of energy from one organism to another.

questions.question: What is a food web?
questions.correct_answer: A network of interconnected food chains.
questions.explanation: Food webs show complex feeding relationships in an ecosystem.

questions.question: What are decomposers?
questions.correct_answer: Organisms that break down dead plants and animals into simpler substances.
questions.explanation: Bacteria and fungi recycle nutrients back into the ecosystem.

questions.question: What is biological magnification?
questions.correct_answer: The increase in concentration of harmful chemicals as they move up the food chain.
questions.explanation: Top consumers accumulate the highest amount of toxic substances.

questions.question: What are biodegradable substances?
questions.correct_answer: Substances that can be decomposed by microorganisms.
questions.explanation: Examples include food waste, paper and plant materials.

questions.question: What are non-biodegradable substances?
questions.correct_answer: Substances that cannot be easily decomposed by microorganisms.
questions.explanation: Examples include plastics, metals and synthetic chemicals.

notes.title: Ecosystem
notes.content: A natural unit consisting of living organisms and their physical environment interacting together.

notes.title: Food Chain
notes.content: A sequence showing how energy is transferred from one organism to another.

notes.title: Food Web
notes.content: A network of interconnected food chains within an ecosystem.

notes.title: Biological Magnification
notes.content: Increase in concentration of toxic substances at higher trophic levels.

notes.title: Biodegradable vs Non-biodegradable
notes.content: Biodegradable materials decompose naturally while non-biodegradable materials persist in the environment.

facts.fact: Green plants are producers because they prepare their own food through photosynthesis.
Energy decreases as it moves from one trophic level to the next.
Biological magnification causes harmful chemicals to accumulate in top consumers.
Biodegradable waste can be broken down naturally by microorganisms.
Plastic is a common non-biodegradable substance that causes environmental pollution.
